name: random_integration_by_batch
label: Random Integration by Batch
summary: Feature values, embedding coordinates, and graph connectivity are all randomly permuted within each batch label
description: Feature values, embedding coordinates, and graph connectivity are all randomly permuted within each batch label

additional_info.paper_name: Open Problems for Single Cell Analysis

name: bbknn
label: BBKNN
summary: BBKNN or batch balanced k nearest neighbours graph is built for each cell by identifying its k nearest neighbours within each defined batch separately, creating independent neighbour sets for each cell in each batch. These sets are then combined and processed with the UMAP algorithm for visualisation.
description: BBKNN or batch balanced k nearest neighbours graph is built for each cell by identifying its k nearest neighbours within each defined batch separately, creating independent neighbour sets for each cell in each batch. These sets are then combined and processed with the UMAP algorithm for visualisation.

additional_info.paper_name: BBKNN: fast batch alignment of single cell transcriptomes

name: random_graph_by_celltype
label: Random Graph by Celltype
summary: Cells are embedded as a one-hot encoding of celltype labels. A graph is then built on this embedding
description: Cells are embedded as a one-hot encoding of celltype labels. A graph is then built on this embedding

additional_info.paper_name: Open Problems for Single Cell Analysis

name: random_integration_by_celltype
label: Random Integration by Celltype
summary: Feature values, embedding coordinates, and graph connectivity are all randomly permuted within each celltype label
description: Feature values, embedding coordinates, and graph connectivity are all randomly permuted within each celltype label

additional_info.paper_name: Open Problems for Single Cell Analysis

name: combat
label: Combat
summary: ComBat uses an Empirical Bayes (EB) approach to correct for batch effects. It estimates batch-specific parameters by pooling information across genes in each batch and shrinks the estimates towards the overall mean of the batch effect estimates across all genes. These parameters are then used to adjust the data for batch effects, leading to more accurate and reproducible results.
description: ComBat uses an Empirical Bayes (EB) approach to correct for batch effects. It estimates batch-specific parameters by pooling information across genes in each batch and shrinks the estimates towards the overall mean of the batch effect estimates across all genes. These parameters are then used to adjust the data for batch effects, leading to more accurate and reproducible results.

additional_info.paper_name: Adjusting batch effects in microarray expression data using empirical Bayes methods

name: fastmnn_embed
label: FastMNN embed
summary: fastMNN performs a multi-sample PCA to reduce dimensionality, identifying MNN paris in the low-dimensional space, and then correcting the target batch towards the reference using locally weighted correction vectors. The corrected target batch is then merged with the reference. The process is repeated with the next target batch except for the PCA step.
description: fastMNN performs a multi-sample PCA to reduce dimensionality, identifying MNN paris in the low-dimensional space, and then correcting the target batch towards the reference using locally weighted correction vectors. The corrected target batch is then merged with the reference. The process is repeated with the next target batch except for the PCA step.

additional_info.paper_name: A description of the theory behind the fastMNN algorithm

name: fastmnn_feature
label: FastMNN feature
summary: fastMNN performs a multi-sample PCA to reduce dimensionality, identifying MNN paris in the low-dimensional space, and then correcting the target batch towards the reference using locally weighted correction vectors. The corrected target batch is then merged with the reference. The process is repeated with the next target batch except for the PCA step.
description: fastMNN performs a multi-sample PCA to reduce dimensionality, identifying MNN paris in the low-dimensional space, and then correcting the target batch towards the reference using locally weighted correction vectors. The corrected target batch is then merged with the reference. The process is repeated with the next target batch except for the PCA step.

additional_info.paper_name: A description of the theory behind the fastMNN algorithm

name: harmony
label: Harmony
summary: Harmony is a method that uses PCA to group the cells into multi-dataset clusters, and then computes cluster-specific linear correction factors. Each cell is then corrected by its cell-specific linear factor using the cluster-weighted average. The method keeps iterating these four steps until cell clusters are stable.
description: Harmony is a method that uses PCA to group the cells into multi-dataset clusters, and then computes cluster-specific linear correction factors. Each cell is then corrected by its cell-specific linear factor using the cluster-weighted average. The method keeps iterating these four steps until cell clusters are stable.

additional_info.paper_name: Fast, sensitive and accurate integration of single-cell data with Harmony

name: liger
label: Liger
summary: LIGER or linked inference of genomic experimental relationships uses iNMF deriving and implementing a novel coordinate descent algorithm to efficiently do the factorization. Joint clustering is performed and factor loadings are normalised.
description: LIGER or linked inference of genomic experimental relationships uses iNMF deriving and implementing a novel coordinate descent algorithm to efficiently do the factorization. Joint clustering is performed and factor loadings are normalised.

additional_info.paper_name: Single-Cell Multi-omic Integration Compares and Contrasts Features of Brain Cell Identity

name: mnn
label: MNN
summary: MNN first detects mutual nearest neighbours in two of the batches and infers a projection of the second onto the first batch. After that, additional batches are added iteratively.
description: MNN first detects mutual nearest neighbours in two of the batches and infers a projection of the second onto the first batch. After that, additional batches are added iteratively.

additional_info.paper_name: Batch effects in single-cell RNA-sequencing data are corrected by matching mutual nearest neighbors

name: no_integration
label: No Integration
summary: Cells are embedded by PCA on the unintegrated data. A graph is built on this PCA embedding.
description: Cells are embedded by PCA on the unintegrated data. A graph is built on this PCA embedding.

additional_info.paper_name: Open Problems for Single Cell Analysis

name: random_integration
label: Random Integration
summary: Feature values, embedding coordinates, and graph connectivity are all randomly permuted
description: Feature values, embedding coordinates, and graph connectivity are all randomly permuted

additional_info.paper_name: Open Problems for Single Cell Analysis

name: scalex
label: SCALEX
summary: SCALEX is a method for integrating heterogeneous single-cell data online using a VAE framework. Its generalised encoder disentangles batch-related components from batch-invariant biological components, which are then projected into a common cell-embedding space.
description: SCALEX is a method for integrating heterogeneous single-cell data online using a VAE framework. Its generalised encoder disentangles batch-related components from batch-invariant biological components, which are then projected into a common cell-embedding space.

additional_info.paper_name: Online single-cell data integration through projecting heterogeneous datasets into a common cell-embedding space

name: scanorama
label: Scanorama
summary: Scanorama is an extension of the MNN method. Other then MNN, it finds mutual nearest neighbours over all batches and embeds observations into a joint hyperplane.
description: Scanorama is an extension of the MNN method. Other then MNN, it finds mutual nearest neighbours over all batches and embeds observations into a joint hyperplane.

additional_info.paper_name: Efficient integration of heterogeneous single-cell transcriptomes using Scanorama

name: scanorama_gene_output
label: Scanorama gene output
summary: Scanorama is an extension of the MNN method. Other then MNN, it finds mutual nearest neighbours over all batches and embeds observations into a joint hyperplane.
description: Scanorama is an extension of the MNN method. Other then MNN, it finds mutual nearest neighbours over all batches and embeds observations into a joint hyperplane.

additional_info.paper_name: Efficient integration of heterogeneous single-cell transcriptomes using Scanorama

name: scanvi
label: scANVI
summary: ScanVI is an extension of scVI but instead using a Bayesian semi-supervised approach for more principled cell annotation.
description: ScanVI is an extension of scVI but instead using a Bayesian semi-supervised approach for more principled cell annotation.

additional_info.paper_name: Probabilistic harmonization and annotation of single‐cell transcriptomics data with deep generative models

name: scvi
label: scVI
summary: scVI combines a variational autoencoder with a hierarchical Bayesian model.
description: scVI combines a variational autoencoder with a hierarchical Bayesian model.

additional_info.paper_name: Deep generative modeling for single-cell transcriptomics
